title: unexpected MultipleTableInfos in rename tables ddl
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
run sql
```
use common_1;
create database common;
create table a (a bigint primary key, b int);
create table b like a;
rename table a to common.c, b to a, common.c to b;
```
<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
| BinlogInfo.MultipleTableInfos[i].ID | BinlogInfo.MultipleTableInfos[i].Name |
| -- | -- |
| **136** | **c** |
| 140 | a |
| 138 | b |
### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
| BinlogInfo.MultipleTableInfos[i].ID | BinlogInfo.MultipleTableInfos[i].Name |
| -- | -- |
| **138** | **b** |
| 140 | a |
| 138 | b |
### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
master
<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE table1 (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, created_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE table2 (id INTEGER, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY (id) REFERENCES table1(id));
INSERT INTO table1 (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Name');
ALTER TABLE table1 RENAME TO renamed_table1;
ALTER TABLE table2 RENAME TO renamed_table2;
key_bug_features: rename table operation causing unexpected MultipleTableInfos
